key_factors: Colombia is ranked 13th in the official FIFA rankings (live 11th), while Ghana is ranked 73rd (live 65th).
Colombia topped Group K with 7 points (wins against Uzbekistan and DR Congo, draw against Portugal), while Ghana advanced as a third-placed wildcard team from Group L with 4 points.
Colombia has no major injury or suspension concerns; Ghana defender Kojo Oppong Peprah is ruled out with a knee injury, but he is a backup/defensive cover rather than a key starting player.
Both teams have exactly 5 rest days since their last group stage match on June 27, and the travel distance difference to Kansas City is under 800 miles (no travel asymmetry).
Extreme heat conditions (daytime temperatures up to 93°F and heat index values up to 110°F) are forecast, but they affect both squads equally.
The referee is French official Clément Turpin, who has issued 2 yellow cards and 0 red cards in his 2 refereed matches this tournament.
unverified_or_unknown: The physical fitness of Antoine Semenyo for Ghana is unconfirmed, as he is managing an ankle injury and undergoes a late fitness test.
The exact starting lineups and tactical approaches are not officially confirmed at prediction time.
